mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with RSAFS month-over-month growth (Pearson r < 0.05) over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8.5% within a three-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer income, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In at least 6 triggered instances, the 20-day return is positive (contradicting the negative direction), or the average return after trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) and accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) together creates a favorable environment for equities: cheap credit and rising output boost corporate profits, pushing S&P 500 higher over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates in 2012 sustained the recovery
failure_condition: Joint signal (low FedFunds + rising INDPRO) produces a mean 60-day return that is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean return (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound (ZLB), persistently low federal funds rates no longer provide additional stimulus and instead signal deep economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in stagnation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% in April 2012, near zero
failure_condition: The average 5-day return during ZLB regimes (identified by FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) is positive or not significantly negative (one-sample t-test vs zero, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A consistent increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects growing manufacturing output, which boosts corporate investment and employment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the economy expands.
failure_condition: The rank IC between 3-month changes in INDPRO and subsequent 60-day returns is below 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 700,000 units, it signals a robust housing market recovery, increasing construction employment and consumer wealth, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After at least 5 trigger events where HOUST exceeds 700, the median 20-day return is not positive, or the proportion of positive returns is not above 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) creates a virtuous cycle of rising incomes and consumption, amplifying positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon beyond the sum of their individual effects.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS growth * RSAFS growth) in a linear regression of 20-day returns has a coefficient not significantly greater than zero (p > 0.10) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation as measured by CPIAUCSL is below 2% year-over-year (low inflation regime), the Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy, supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 229.177 in April 2012 (approx 2.7% yoy, above 2%?)
failure_condition: During months when CPIAUCSL yoy < 2%, the mean 60-day return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the regime has fewer than 10 non-overlapping observations.
